steps.title: Multiply the binomials using distribution
steps.content:
Expand $\left(\frac{3}{5}x + \frac{7}{6}\right)(6x - 5)$ by multiplying each term in the first binomial by each term in the second:
$$\frac{3}{5}x \cdot 6x + \frac{3}{5}x \cdot (-5) + \frac{7}{6} \cdot 6x + \frac{7}{6} \cdot (-5)$$
Simplify each product:
$$\frac{18}{5}x^2 - 3x + 7x - \frac{35}{6}$$

steps.title: Combine like terms and identify the choice
steps.content:
Combine the linear terms: $-3x + 7x = 4x$.
$$\frac{18}{5}x^2 + 4x - \frac{35}{6}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
